results.title: AdDojo: Go from raw footage to viral ads in seconds | Product Hunt
results.content:
We built Addojo because we were all drowning in the same problem - having great footage but no time to turn it into content that actually performed.  
  
Aryan and I have been friends since high school in Dubai, working on software for autism centers. When we ended up at CMU's TartanHacks, we met Erik (who Aryan knew from Brown after he transferred from UW) and Victor (who Erik knew from their Washington days). It's funny how these connections work - four people from completely different paths ending up in the same room, all frustrated by the same thing. [...] Lyss Luo

This product idea is brilliant! I especially love that you're not trying to generate fake content, but rather helping us find "the moments that truly matter" from our real footage. This is exactly what creators need. The ability to understand the differences between various types of content and adjust accordingly shows a deep understanding of user needs. I'm excited to see how this can change my content creation workflow!

results.title: Photo sharing Reviewed & Ranked for 2026 | Product Hunt
results.content:
I grew up during the golden era of Instagram, back in 2012 2015, when your feed was just photos from your friends in the order they posted them. No ads interrupting the scroll. No algorithm deciding what you should see. No pressure to make reels or go viral. Just real moments, shared simply. That version of social media made me fall in love with photography  
Infotograf gives you a simple chronological feed, beautiful film-inspired filters, and ambient audio captured with every photo so you can hear the moment, not just see it. It connects to the Fediverse through ActivityPub, which means your photos aren't trapped in any one company's ecosystem. You own everything you post. [...] Support our launch 

DUONG NGUYEN

it's an iOS photo sharing app that takes you back to when social media was actually about in 2015 ,sharing moments with friends. Before the ads, the algorithms, and the endless video reels took over. [...] # Photo sharing Reviewed & Ranked for 2026

Discover tools to capture, organize, and share visuals—from photos and screenshots to posts. Ideal for creators, teams, and fans seeking inspiration or portfolios.

community building (14)content sharing (7)news feed (2) [...] 4.4

Based on 37 reviews

Review Facebook?

Reviewers mostly see Facebook as a practical, familiar place to keep up with friends, family, groups, and local communities, with Marketplace, messaging, Pages, and business tools mentioned often. Many praise its accessible interface across mobile and desktop, though several say the experience now feels cluttered by ads, sponsored posts, and algorithm-driven feeds. The sharpest criticism centers on privacy, security, misinformation, bots, and fake AI content. One founder of Layers also notes that its makers use Facebook Login for quick, familiar sign-in.

results.title: SnoopReport Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
Report

 4 views

•2 reviews

I'm so glad I found this tool! It's a great alternative to traditional parental controls. Unlike Instagram's built-in controls or Qustodio, it won't make you feel like you're being too harsh or overprotective. It doesn't restrict your child's access to Instagram content or limit their phone usage. It's also a great way to learn about your child's interests based on their likes and follows.

Report

 9 views

•2 reviews

#### What's great

Well, while this is a great product, it was sad to know most well-known public figures don't like anything on Instagram, I really wanted to check what they like. Nice tool to check what my friends do on their pages though, thanks for that

Ratings

Ease of use

Reliability

Report

 8 views

•6 reviews [...] Based on 20 reviews

Review SnoopReport?

Reviewers say SnoopReport is useful when it works: people use it to watch friends, celebrities, competitors, children, or dating prospects, and they like the simple reports, past activity access, and AI-style summaries of interests based on likes, follows, and hashtags. Makers of products like Nora and StockAlgos.com - Leaderboard also describe it as handy for audience research and content ideas. But negative reviews are sharp, citing missed or partial activity, empty reports, limits on private or larger accounts, and poor support.

Hi friends 👋,

I'm sorry. Soshi will not be launching today.

Honestly, this was an incredibly tough decision. Mike and I were nervous sending this email afraid we'd lose momentum and even your support.

Our team has been working tirelessly over the past few weeks, but the product isn't exactly up to the standards we want for you

However, I think this is ultimately the right decision.
